paper.abstract_en: In this work, we propose a new scan tool that automatically calculates maximal cross section predictions for a new physics scenario with additional scalar states. While the tool is currently optimized for asymmetric production and decay processes in the form of p p -> h3 -> h1 h2, where hi denote CP even neutral scalars, in principle it can be extended to include any production and decay mode. As the code builds largely on the structure of the publicly available code ScannerS, it can in principle be extended to any other model contained within this tool. We here show first applications for a specific model, the Two-Real-Singlet Model (TRSM), and compare to publicly available results from the LHC collaborations from the previous runs. We also in detail discuss the structure of the code, including code installation, usage, as well as underlying algorithms.
